title: Unit, Integration, CLI, Fixture, and Changed-Case Testing · Program Brief: Cli Subprocess
summary: Tests assert only one public-domain English sample total, mock every filesystem call, compare unordered dictionaries, share mutable fixtures, and never run the installed command. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cli subprocess increment in risk-layered Bookbot test suite. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Test stdout, stderr, status, help, invalid arguments, working directory, and installed entry point as observable CLI behavior.
Use real temporary files and directories for filesystem contracts and reserve mocks for narrow failure injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-cli-subprocess — Test stdout, stderr, status, help, invalid arguments, working directory, and installed entry point as observable CLI behavior.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-testing-fixtures-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-testing-fixtures-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_testing_fixtures_4(unit_cases: int = 18, integration_cases: int = 7, cli_cases: int = 8, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, installed_smoke: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, integration_cases, cli_cases) < 1:
        return False, 0, "test-layers"
    if integration_cases < 2:
        return False, integration_cases, "filesystem-evidence"
    if cli_cases < 2:
        return False, cli_cases, "cli-evidence"
    if not changed_defect_detected or not installed_smoke:
        return False, unit_cases, "weak-suite"
    total = unit_cases + integration_cases + cli_cases
    assert total >= 7
    return True, total, "risk-layered-tests"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test stdout, stderr, status, help, invalid arguments, working directory, and installed entry point as observable CLI behavior.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Cli Subprocess model · Theory Cli Subprocess, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Cli Subprocess precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Cli Subprocess model · Theory Cli Subprocess, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 7736 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
